(born 15 December 1970) is a French professional [ski instructor](/wiki/Skiing "Skiing"), competitive [ski mountaineer](/wiki/Ski_mountaineer "Ski mountaineer"),[[1]](#cite_note-ski-mount-1) and champion [mountain runner](/wiki/Fell_running "Fell running").[[2]](#cite_note-tenforme-2)

["Ski Mountaineering 2006 - Full Results + World Ranking"](http://www.uiaa.ch/web.test/visual/SkiMount/Results%20and%20Rankings/result06/Cuneo/Classifica%20ufficiale%20Relay%20SeniorFem.pdf) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20070927193037/http://www.uiaa.ch/web.test/visual/SkiMount/Results%20and%20Rankings/result06/Cuneo/Classifica%20ufficiale%20Relay%20SeniorFem.pdf) 2007-09-27 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), UIAA

[Profile of Corinne Favre](http://www.tenforme.com/tenforme/themes/sportifs/raid.htm?id=12) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20071009125331/http://www.tenforme.com/tenforme/themes/sportifs/raid.htm?id=12) 2007-10-09 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), Tenforme.com
[*"Grave accident pour Corinne Favre sur le Pumori (Népal)"*](http://www.kairn.com/news_montagne_65886.grave-accident-pour-corinne-favre-pumori-nepal.html), Kairn.com, November 5, 2008 (French)

[*"Championnat de France Vertical Race"*](http://www.skimountaineering.org/en/3101-championnat-de-france-vertical-race-fr) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20110721102322/http://www.skimountaineering.org/en/3101-championnat-de-france-vertical-race-fr) 2011-07-21 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), skimountaineering.org, January 31, 2009 (French)

French ski instructor, ski mountaineer, and mountain runner

**Corinne Favre** (born 15 December 1970) is a French professional [ski instructor](/wiki/Skiing "Skiing"), competitive [ski mountaineer](/wiki/Ski_mountaineer "Ski mountaineer"),[[1]](#cite_note-ski-mount-1) and champion [mountain runner](/wiki/Fell_running "Fell running").[[2]](#cite_note-tenforme-2)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

In November 2008 she and an accompanying [Sherpa](/wiki/Sherpa_people "Sherpa people") suffered a serious accident while descending from the [Pumori](/wiki/Pumori "Pumori") mountain during an expedition in [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal").[[3]](#cite_note-kairn-3) After convalescing, she returned to action in the *Championnat de France Vertical Race* in January 2009.[[4]](#cite_note-fr_vertical-4)

## Selected results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Selected results")]

### Ski mountaineering

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ski mountaineering")]

* 1998:
  + 3rd, *Trofeo Kima*[[5]](#cite_note-trofeo_kima-5)
* 1999:
  + 1st, Tour du Rutor (together with [Gloriana Pellissier](/wiki/Gloriana_Pellissier "Gloriana Pellissier"))[[6]](#cite_note-Tour_du_Rutor-6)
  + 3rd, French national ranking[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* 2001:
  + 3rd, French national ranking[[8]](#cite_note-8)
  + 4th, [European Championship](/wiki/2001_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2001 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") (team race together with [Nathalie Bourillon](/wiki/Nathalie_Bourillon "Nathalie Bourillon"))
* 2002:
  + 1st, *Trofeo Kima*[[5]](#cite_note-trofeo_kima-5)
  + 1st, Tour du Rutor (together with [Carole Toïgo](/wiki/Carole_To%C3%AFgo "Carole Toïgo"))[[6]](#cite_note-Tour_du_Rutor-6)
  + 2nd, [World Championship](/wiki/2002_World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2002 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") (team race)
  + 4th, World Championship combined ranking
  + 5th, World Championship single race
* 2003:
  + 3rd, [European Championship](/wiki/2003_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2003 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") (team race together with [Véronique Lathuraz](/wiki/V%C3%A9ronique_Lathuraz "Véronique Lathuraz"))
  + 5th, European Championship (single race)
  + 5th, European Championship (combined ranking)
* 2004:
  + 1st, *Trofeo Kima*[[5]](#cite_note-trofeo_kima-5)
  + 7th, [World Championship](/wiki/2004_World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2004 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") (combined ranking)
  + 10th, World Championship single race
* 2006:
  + 1st, *Trofeo Kima*[[5]](#cite_note-trofeo_kima-5)
  + 3rd, [World Championship](/wiki/2006_World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2006 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") team race (together with Carole Toïgo)
  + 3rd, World Championship relay race (together with Carole Toïgo, Véronique Lathuraz and Nathalie Bourillon)
  + 5th, World Championship [vertical race](/wiki/Vertical_race "Vertical race")
* 2007:
  + 1st, *Trofeo Kima*[[5]](#cite_note-trofeo_kima-5)
  + 2nd, [European Championship](/wiki/2007_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2007 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") team race (together with Véronique Lathuraz)
  + 2nd, European Championship relay race (together with Véronique Lathuraz and [Laëtitia Roux](/wiki/La%C3%ABtitia_Roux "Laëtitia Roux"))
  + 7th, European Championship vertical race
* 2008:
  + 3rd, [World Championship](/wiki/2008_World_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2008 World Championship of Ski Mountaineering") relay race (together with Nathalie Bourillon, Véronique Lathuraz and [Valentine Fabre](/wiki/Valentine_Fabre "Valentine Fabre"))
  + 4th, World Championship team race (together with Nathalie Bourillon)
  + 5th, World Championship vertical race
  + 10th, World Championship combined ranking
* 2009:
  + 3rd, [European Championship](/wiki/2009_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2009 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") relay race (together with Véronique Lathuraz and Laëtitia Roux)
* 2012:
  + 4th, [European Championship](/wiki/2012_European_Championship_of_Ski_Mountaineering "2012 European Championship of Ski Mountaineering") relay, together with [Émilie Favre](/wiki/%C3%89milie_Favre "Émilie Favre") and Laëtitia Roux
  + 7th, European Championship vertical race

#### Pierra Menta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Pierra Menta")]

Main article: [Pierra Menta](/wiki/Pierra_Menta "Pierra Menta")

* 1997: 4th, together with Nathalie Bourillon
* 1998: 3rd, together with [Jana Heczková](/wiki/Jana_Heczkov%C3%A1 "Jana Heczková")
* 1999: 2nd, together with [Gloriana Pellissier](/wiki/Gloriana_Pellissier "Gloriana Pellissier")
* 2001: 2nd, together with Carole Toïgo
* 2003: 3rd, together with Carole Toïgo
* 2004: 2nd, together with Carole Toïgo
* 2005: 3rd, together with Carole Toïgo
* 2006: 4th, together with Carole Toïgo
* 2007: 2nd, together with Nathalie Bourillon
* 2008: 4th, together with Véronique Lathuraz
* 2009: 5th, together with Magali Jacquemoud

#### Patrouille des Glaciers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Patrouille des Glaciers")]

Main article: [Patrouille des Glaciers](/wiki/Patrouille_des_Glaciers "Patrouille des Glaciers")

* 1998: 3rd, together with [Danielle Hacquard](/wiki/Danielle_Hacquard "Danielle Hacquard") and Véronique Lathuraz
* 2006: 2nd (together with Véronique Lathuraz and Nathalie Bourillon)
* 2008: 2nd (together with Laëtitia Roux and Nathalie Bourillon)

#### Trofeo Mezzalama

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Trofeo Mezzalama")]

Main article: [Trofeo Mezzalama](/wiki/Trofeo_Mezzalama "Trofeo Mezzalama")

* 2007: 2nd, together with Véronique Lathuraz and Nathalie Bourillon
* 2011: 4th, together with [Gabrielle Magnenat](/wiki/Gabrielle_Magnenat "Gabrielle Magnenat") and [Émilie Gex-Fabry](/wiki/%C3%89milie_Gex-Fabry "Émilie Gex-Fabry")[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### Sky running

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Corinne_Favre&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sky running")]

Favre won the [Sentiero 4 Luglio SkyMarathon](/wiki/Sentiero_4_Luglio_SkyMarathon "Sentiero 4 Luglio SkyMarathon") in 2002, 2003, 2007 and 2008. In 2005, she won the [Pikes Peak Marathon](/wiki/Pikes_Peak_Marathon "Pikes Peak Marathon") at her first attempt,[[10]](#cite_note-pikes_2005-10) and also won the [Buff Skyrunner World Series](/wiki/Buff_Skyrunner_World_Series "Buff Skyrunner World Series") the same year after winning five of the six races in which she competed.[[11]](#cite_note-buff2005-11) She had previously finished second in 2003 despite winning three races, more than any other [competitor](/wiki/Competition "Competition"),[[12]](#cite_note-buff2003-12) and seventh overall in 2004 after placing first and second in the only two races in which she took part.[[13]](#cite_note-buff2004-13) She won her second Skyrunner Series title in 2008,[[14]](#cite_note-buff-08-14) and is also a three-time winner of the race from [Base Camp](/wiki/Everest_Base_Camp "Everest Base Camp") to [Lukla](/wiki/Lukla "Lukla") on [Mount Everest](/wiki/Mount_Everest "Mount Everest").[[15]](#cite_note-everest-15)
